Selected work

Case studies

Case study 01

Message Capping NUX

Meta · WhatsApp Business · Shipped March 2026

$1B+ product line

Content leadership Cross-functional Monetization NUX

Problem

WhatsApp was introducing chat cap limits. Four teams disagreed on how to frame it. Early drafts sold the cap as a "new" feature or leaned on judgmental language like "unwanted messages."

What I did

Reframed the limits as a consumer-protection standard, not a value prop. Documented the rationale for each word choice: "overload" over "unwanted," "haven't responded to you" over "never messaged you." Proposed a compromise half-sheet with a toast that resolved 53 open review comments without escalation.

Outcome

My positioning guidance was adopted as GTM's standard. Capping contributed to Business Messaging Monetization's 170% incremental revenue increase and 7x monthly revenue lift.

+170%
Incremental revenue increase
7×
Monthly revenue lift
53
Review comments resolved without escalation

Early drafts: 5 variants reviewed

Preferred by CD

Final draft

Shipped: consumer warning + NUX

Case study 02

Dynamic Audiences Naming

Meta · WhatsApp Business · Shipped April 2026

$1B+ product line

Naming systems Information architecture 74 countries

Problem

SMB users navigated jargon like "Set as dynamic audience" and table-formatted descriptions just to reach their own customers.

What I did

Created the product-wide audience naming standard. Replaced legacy labels with human-centric terms: "Active chats," "Inactive chats," "Your WhatsApp contacts." Named the "Automatic updates" toggle, wrote snackbar copy for both states, and flagged an active/inactive overlap bug where the same 30-day cutoff caused a contact to appear in both lists.

Outcome

Naming became the standard across all audience surfaces. Shipped in 74 countries.

4.3×
Picker-to-Composer conversion
1.4×
Composer-to-Draft conversion
−28.6%
Contact picker time (M1)

Naming exploration: before & variants

Shipped: new proposed flows

Case study 03

GenAI Content Strategy

Meta · WhatsApp Business · Shipped May 2026

GenAI Image Composer

GenAI Content strategy Terminology NUX

Problem

WhatsApp Business was integrating GenAI features. Technical terms like "generate" and "regenerate" didn't match the plain language users expect. The NUX had to introduce AI without overpromising or confusing.

What I did

Standardized terminology, replacing "generate" with "create" across all GenAI surfaces. Resolved the ambiguous "From a message" CTA, landing on "Use a message" / "Use a photo" pill labels and a "Create AI images" screen title.

Outcome

The "create" terminology was adopted as the standard across GenAI surfaces, with all improvements statistically significant.

+22.8%
Messages delivered
+26.1%
Messages read
+25.6%
Messages replied

Education & NUX: before, preferred, and shipped

G Suite subscription flow

Google

Cancellation messaging

Rewrote cancel-flow copy to separate downgrade benefits from cancellation consequences, with clearer CTAs that drove more users to downgrade instead of canceling outright.

Higher downgrade conversion

Charles Schwab

Advisor-client trading UI

Designed dual-view content for margin and options trading: advisors see full strategy details, clients see only what's been selected, reducing trading errors and improving trust.

Fewer trading errors

Charles Schwab

Error message rewrite

Simplified an overly apologetic, multi-line 404 into two words and one sentence. Shifted ownership: "Can't find page" (our issue) became "Page not found" (accurate framing).

Reduced cognitive load

Google

String review at scale

Set a goal of 750 strings reviewed in 3 months. Built a scalable TextID-based workflow and reviewed 3,500+ strings, 4× the target, without changing underlying code.

3,500+ strings · scalable process

Apple

Email preferences redesign

Rewrote a cryptic "Communication Preferences Update" email, surfacing the change upfront, adding deep links and support article references, to reduce support tickets and build trust.

Fewer support tickets

Content Design & Strategy roles

Over 15 years of experience in UX writing, content strategy, and information architecture across enterprise, consumer, and fintech products.

I'm a Senior Content Designer and UX Writer with a career spanning Meta, Apple, Google, Schwab, and beyond. I specialize in high-stakes, regulated, and monetization-sensitive environments where the words have to earn their place.

My work lives at the intersection of clarity, systems thinking, and user trust. I believe the best content decisions come from understanding the whole product, not just the string.

When I'm not writing, I'm at Stinson Beach, walking my beagle Princess Leia, or sharpening my storytelling at Toastmasters.

Let's work together.

Open to Senior / Staff Content Designer roles in the San Francisco Bay Area and beyond.
